curriculum.subject: Physics
curriculum.gradeLevel: Class XI / Class XII / 12th Grade / Science
curriculum.overallDescription: 19 units, 28 chapters, and 2 topics extracted through raw extraction, document-structure hierarchy building, node enrichment, normalized teaching blocks, validation, and statistics generation.
coreObjectives: Identify the need for measurement and distinguish between fundamental and derived units within various systems of units.
Apply SI units, symbols, and nomenclature as per international standards in physical contexts.
Determine significant figures and calculate uncertainties in experimental results.
Perform dimensional analysis to derive relationships between physical quantities and verify the correctness of equations.
Describe motion in a straight line using elementary concepts of differentiation and integration.
Distinguish between average speed, average velocity, instantaneous velocity, uniform, and non-uniform motion.
Analyze motion graphs (velocity-time and position-time) to interpret kinematic behavior.
Derive and apply relations for uniformly accelerated motion using both graphical methods and calculus.
Differentiate between scalar and vector quantities and perform addition, subtraction, and multiplication (scalar/vector product) of vectors.
Resolve vectors into rectangular components in a plane using unit vectors.
Analyze cases of uniform velocity and uniform acceleration for projectile motion and uniform circular motion.
Explain the intuitive concept of force, inertia, and formulate Newton's three laws of motion including impulse.

topics: Need for measurement, Units of measurement, Systems of units

question_paper_design.description: Exhibit memory of previously learned material by recalling facts, terms, basic concepts, and answers.
Demonstrate understanding of facts and ideas by organizing, comparing, translating, interpreting, giving descriptions, and stating main ideas.
Solve problems to new situations by applying acquired knowledge, facts, techniques and rules in a different way.

Examine and break information into parts by identifying motives or causes. Make inferences and find evidence to support generalizations; Present and defend opinions by making judgments about information, validity of ideas, or quality of work based on a set of criteria; Compile information together in a different way by combining elements in a new pattern or proposing alternative solutions.

question_paper_design.description: Exhibit memory of previously learned material by recalling facts, terms, basic concepts, and answers.
Demonstrate understanding of facts and ideas by organizing, comparing, translating, interpreting, giving descriptions, and stating main ideas.
Solve problems to new situations by applying acquired knowledge, facts, techniques and rules in a different way.

Examine and break information into parts by identifying motives or causes. Make inferences and find evidence to support generalizations; Present and defend opinions by making judgments about information, validity of ideas, or quality of work based on a set of criteria; Compile information together in a different way by combining elements in a new pattern or proposing alternative solutions.

competency_groups.title: Physical World and Measurement - Units and Measurements
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to measurement systems, SI units, significant figures, dimensional analysis, and uncertainty determination.
competencies: Identify the need for measurement and distinguish between fundamental and derived units within various systems of units.
Apply SI units, symbols, and nomenclature as per international standards in physical contexts.
Determine significant figures and calculate uncertainties in experimental results.
Perform dimensional analysis to derive relationships between physical quantities and verify the correctness of equations.

competency_groups.title: Kinematics - Motion in a Straight Line
competency_groups.description: Competencies involving the description of motion using calculus, graphical analysis, and equations for uniformly accelerated motion.
competencies: Describe motion in a straight line using elementary concepts of differentiation and integration.
Distinguish between average speed, average velocity, instantaneous velocity, uniform, and non-uniform motion.
Analyze motion graphs (velocity-time and position-time) to interpret kinematic behavior.
Derive and apply relations for uniformly accelerated motion using both graphical methods and calculus.

competency_groups.title: Kinematics - Motion in a Plane
competency_groups.description: Competencies covering vector operations, resolution of vectors, and analysis of projectile and circular motion.
competencies: Differentiate between scalar and vector quantities and perform addition, subtraction, and multiplication (scalar/vector product) of vectors.
Resolve vectors into rectangular components in a plane using unit vectors.
Analyze cases of uniform velocity and uniform acceleration for projectile motion and uniform circular motion.

competency_groups.title: Laws of Motion - Dynamics and Equilibrium
competency_groups.description: Competencies focused on Newton's laws, conservation of momentum, friction, and dynamics of circular motion.
competencies: Explain the intuitive concept of force, inertia, and formulate Newton's three laws of motion including impulse.
Apply the law of conservation of linear momentum to solve collision problems.
Analyze equilibrium conditions for concurrent forces and distinguish between static, kinetic, and rolling friction.
Derive expressions for centripetal force in uniform circular motion (e.g., vehicles on level/banked roads).

competency_groups.title: Work, Energy and Power - Conservation Principles
competency_groups.description: Competencies involving work-energy theorem, potential energy concepts, conservative forces, and collision analysis.
competencies: Calculate work done by constant and variable forces and relate it to kinetic energy via the work-energy theorem.
Distinguish between conservative and non-conservative forces and define potential energy (including spring systems).
Analyze power as a rate of doing work and apply conservation principles in vertical circular motion.
Evaluate elastic and inelastic collisions in one and two dimensions using momentum and energy conservation.

competency_groups.title: System of Particles and Rotational Motion - Dynamics
competency_groups.description: Competencies covering center of mass, torque, angular momentum conservation, and rotational dynamics.
competencies: Determine the center of mass for two-particle systems and rigid bodies (e.g., uniform rod).
Apply laws of conservation of linear and angular momentum to system problems.
Analyze equilibrium conditions for rigid bodies under concurrent forces and torques.
Compare linear and rotational motions, calculating moment of inertia and radius of gyration for simple geometrical objects.
competency_groups.unit_name: Motion of System of Particles and Rigid Body
competency_groups.chapter_name: System of Particles and Rotational Motion

competency_groups.title: Gravitation - Planetary and Satellite Dynamics
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to gravitational laws, potential energy, and orbital mechanics.
competencies: Apply Kepler's laws of planetary motion and Newton's universal law of gravitation.
Analyze the variation of acceleration due to gravity with altitude and depth.
Calculate gravitational potential energy, escape speed, and orbital velocity for satellites.

competency_groups.title: Properties of Bulk Matter - Solids and Fluids
competency_groups.description: Competencies involving elasticity, stress-strain relationships, fluid dynamics, surface tension, and viscosity.
competencies: Apply Hooke's law to determine elastic moduli (Young’s, bulk, shear) and calculate elastic energy in solids.
Analyze pressure distribution in fluids using Pascal's law and Bernoulli's theorem.
Explain phenomena related to viscosity, terminal velocity, streamline/turbulent flow, surface tension, and capillary action.

competency_groups.title: Properties of Bulk Matter - Fluids (Continued)
competency_groups.description: Competencies focusing on fluid properties, pressure variations, and surface phenomena.
competencies: Calculate excess pressure across curved surfaces and explain applications in drops, bubbles, and capillary rise.

competency_groups.title: Properties of Bulk Matter - Thermal Properties
competency_groups.description: Competencies covering heat transfer, specific heats, and thermal radiation laws.
competencies: Distinguish between temperature and heat, explaining anomalous expansion of water.

competency_groups.title: Thermodynamics - Laws and Processes
competency_groups.description: Competencies involving thermodynamic laws, state variables, and process analysis.
competencies: Apply the Zeroth law to define thermal equilibrium and temperature.

competency_groups.title: Kinetic Theory - Gas Behavior
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to the kinetic theory of gases, molecular speeds, and degrees of freedom.
competencies: Derive or apply the equation of state for a perfect gas.
competency_groups.unit_name: Behaviour of Perfect Gases and Kinetic Theory of Gases

competency_groups.title: Oscillations - Simple Harmonic Motion
competency_groups.description: Competencies covering periodic motion, SHM equations, energy in oscillation, and pendulums.
competencies: Analyze simple harmonic motion (S.H.M.) including phase, restoring force, and energy conservation.

competency_groups.title: Waves - Propagation and Superposition
competency_groups.description: Competencies involving wave types, superposition principle, standing waves, and beats.
competencies: Distinguish between transverse and longitudinal waves and describe their propagation.

competency_groups.title: Electrostatics - Charges and Fields
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to Coulomb's law, electric fields, dipoles, flux, and Gauss's theorem.
competencies: Apply Coulomb's law and the superposition principle for point charges.

competency_groups.title: Electrostatic Potential and Capacitance - Energy Storage
competency_groups.description: Competencies involving electric potential, equipotential surfaces, dielectrics, and capacitor networks.
competencies: Calculate electric potential due to point charges and dipoles.

competency_groups.title: Current Electricity - Circuits and Laws
competency_groups.description: Competencies covering drift velocity, Ohm's law, resistivity, Kirchhoff's rules, and Wheatstone bridge.
competencies: Relate electric current to drift velocity and mobility.

competency_groups.title: Magnetic Effects - Fields and Forces
competency_groups.description: Competencies involving Biot-Savart law, Ampere's law, forces on charges/conductors, and magnetic dipoles.
competencies: Apply the Biot-Savart law to calculate fields from current loops.
competency_groups.unit_name: Unit-III Magnetic Effects of Current and Magnetism

competency_groups.title: Magnetism - Materials and Properties
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to magnetic properties of materials, magnetization, and temperature effects.
competencies: Classify substances as para-, dia-, or ferro-magnetic based on their response to external fields.
competency_groups.unit_name: Unit-III Magnetic Effects of Current and Magnetism

competency_groups.title: Electromagnetic Induction - Laws and Applications
competency_groups.description: Competencies covering Faraday's laws, Lenz's law, self/mutual induction.
competencies: Apply Faraday's laws of electromagnetic induction to determine induced EMF.
competency_groups.unit_name: Unit-IV Electromagnetic Induction and Alternating Currents

competency_groups.title: Alternating Current - AC Circuits
competency_groups.description: Competencies involving RMS values, reactance, impedance, resonance, and power in AC circuits.
competencies: Analyze LCR series circuits using phasors to determine resonance conditions.
competency_groups.unit_name: Unit-IV Electromagnetic Induction and Alternating Currents

competency_groups.title: Electromagnetic Waves - Spectrum and Nature
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to the nature of EM waves, displacement current, and the electromagnetic spectrum.
competencies: Describe the transverse nature of electromagnetic waves.

competency_groups.title: Optics - Ray Optics and Instruments
competency_groups.description: Competencies covering reflection, refraction, lenses, mirrors, optical instruments, and dispersion.
competencies: Apply mirror formulae and lens maker's formula to solve image formation problems.

competency_groups.title: Optics - Wave Optics
competency_groups.description: Competencies involving Huygen's principle, interference, diffraction, and coherence.
competencies: Explain the phenomenon of interference using Young's double slit experiment.

competency_groups.title: Dual Nature - Photoelectric Effect and Matter Waves
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to the particle nature of light, photoelectric equation, and de-Broglie relation.
competencies: Explain experimental observations of the photoelectric effect using Einstein's equation.
competency_groups.unit_name: Unit–VII Dual Nature of Radiation and Matter
competency_groups.chapter_name: Dual Nature of Radiation and Matter

competency_groups.title: Atoms - Models and Spectra
competency_groups.description: Competencies covering Rutherford's model, Bohr's postulates, hydrogen spectra.
competencies: Describe the limitations of Rutherford's model and explain Bohr's quantization conditions.

competency_groups.title: Nuclei - Structure and Reactions
competency_groups.description: Competencies involving nuclear composition, binding energy, fission, and fusion.
competencies: Analyze the variation of binding energy per nucleon with mass number.

competency_groups.title: Electronic Devices - Semiconductors and Diodes
competency_groups.description: Competencies covering energy bands, p-n junctions, rectification.
competencies: Distinguish between intrinsic and extrinsic semiconductors (p-type and n-type).

question_paper_design.description: Exhibit memory of previously learned material by recalling facts, terms, basic concepts, and answers.
Demonstrate understanding of facts and ideas by organizing, comparing, translating, interpreting, giving descriptions, and stating main ideas.
Solve problems to new situations by applying acquired knowledge, facts, techniques and rules in a different way.

Examine and break information into parts by identifying motives or causes. Make inferences and find evidence to support generalizations; Present and defend opinions by making judgments about information, validity of ideas, or quality of work based on a set of criteria; Compile information together in a different way by combining elements in a new pattern or proposing alternative solutions.

suggested_projects_class_xii: To study various factors on which the internal resistance/EMF of a cell depends.

To study the variations in current flowing in a circuit containing an LDR because of a variation in (a) the power of the incandescent lamp, used to 'illuminate' the LDR. (b) the distance of an incandescent lamp (of fixed power).
To find the refractive indices of water and oil using a plane mirror, an equiconvex lens and an adjustable object needle.
To investigate the relation between the ratio of output and input voltage and number of turns in secondary/primary coil of a self-designed transformer.
To investigate the dependence of angle of deviation on angle of incidence using a hollow prism filled with different transparent fluids.
To estimate the charge induced on each one of two identical Styrofoam (or pith) balls suspended in a vertical plane by making use of Coulomb's law.
To study the factor on which self-inductance of a coil depends by observing its effect when put in series with a resistor/bulb in an AC circuit fed up by adjustable frequency source.
To study earth's magnetic field using compass needle-bar magnet by plotting magnetic field lines and tangent galvanometer.
